text:
Admission Date:  2008-08-10              Discharge Date:   1980-01-01

Date of Birth:  1983-08-03             Sex:   F

Service: CARDIOTHORACIC

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Alice
Chief Complaint:
severe dyspnea

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
2008-08-10: Right thoracotomy and thoracic tracheoplasty with
mesh, right mainstem bronchus/bronchus intermedius bronchoplasty
with mesh, left mainstem bronchoplasty with mesh, right upper
lobe wedge resection.


History of Present Illness:
Ms. Edwards is a 59-year-old woman with severe dyspnea. She
has emphysema but on further workup was noted to have severe
diffuse tracheobronchomalacia. She underwent a tracheobronchial
silicone Y stent trial and had marked improvement of her
symptoms. Due to these improved symptoms, she was offered
surgical intervention. Due to a nodule seen on the CT scan,
wedge resection was also offered to the patient.

Past Medical History:
OSA
COPD with CPAP, on home O2
Tracheomalacia
Tonsillectomy
Back surgery
Appendectomy


Social History:
remote smoking history

Family History:
non-contributory

Physical Exam:
On discharge:

vitals: 99 98 88 96/54 18 95% 2L NC

NAD
Distant heart sounds, RRR, S1/S2
Scattered Rhonchi
Abdom soft, non-tender
Thoracotomy site clean, dry, intact. Chest tube site intact.
Trace edema ankle bilaterally
Tolerating PO


Pertinent Results:
CXR: 2002-05-08

FINDINGS: In comparison with the study of 02-14, the apparent
spinal stimulator has been removed. There is progressive
decrease in the subcutaneous emphysema on the right. Mild
basilar atelectatic changes are seen. There is no evidence of
acute focal pneumonia or vascular congestion.


1980-01-01 03:10PM BLOOD WBC-6.3 RBC-3.36* Hgb-10.7* Hct-32.1*
MCV-95 MCH-31.7 MCHC-33.2 RDW-13.6 Plt Ct-341
1980-01-01 03:10PM BLOOD Plt Ct-341
2011-05-12 07:00AM BLOOD Glucose-108* UreaN-10 Creat-0.5 Na-140
K-3.7 Cl-102 HCO3-28 AnGap-14

Brief Hospital Course:
Anethesia placed a pre-operative epidural on 10-24. After on
uncomplicated right thoracotomy and thoracic tracheoplasty with
mesh, right mainstem bronchus/bronchus intermedius bronchoplasty
with mesh, left mainstem bronchoplasty with mesh and right upper
lobe wedge resection, the patient was admitted to the surgical
ICU for further treatment. Due to rhoncherous nature of breath
sounds and the use of oxygen 15 liters by mist mask, the patient
remained in the ICU until 06-25. No bronchoscopy was required.
Chest tube was removed on 03-13.  The epidural was removed on 02-14.
PT saw the patient and ambulated on 02-14.

Due to prolonged nature of the surgery, CPK values were trended
over the first 3 days post-op. On POD1, values were 5014 and
trended downward for the next 2 days. The patient required CPAP
(14 cm H20) while on the floor.

No blood transfusions were required during the hospitalization.
At the time of discharge, the patient was afebrile, without a
WBC count, and saturating well on 2 liters NC.

Due to patient preferences and deconditioning, the patient
requested continued care in a rehabilitation setting. The
patient was discharge on 1980-01-01

tiotropium bromide-dose uncertain
advair-dosage uncertain

aspirin 81 mg Highlands Behavioral Health System

Truvada Eye Drops-dosage unknown. apply to R eye daily

Facility:

Lake Michaelside, ME 10551

Discharge Diagnosis:

COPD on home 02


Discharge Condition:

Discharge Instructions:
Call Dr. Anderson 596-99-7625 if experience:
-Fever > 101 or chills
-Increased shortness of breath or cough
-Chest pain
-Incision develops drainage
You may shower: No tub bathing or swimming for 6 weeks
No driving while taking narcotics:  Take stool softners with
narcotics.
Oxygen 1-3Liter via nasal cannula as needed.
CPAP at bedtime


Followup Instructions:
Follow-up with Dr. Anderson 2011-11-05:30am on the Emergency Department
Building Highlands Behavioral Health System I 6887 Morgan Stream
Lake Michaelside, ME 10551 Chest Disease Center
Report to the ICU 6887 Morgan Stream
Lake Michaelside, ME 10551 Radiology Department for a
Chest X-Ray 45 minutes before your appointment.
